plainEnglish: At Level AAA, changes of context—navigation, new windows, major content swaps—happen only when the user explicitly requests them, or you provide a way to turn automatic changes off. No surprise redirects, auto-refreshes, or pop-ups.
whyItMatters: Users with cognitive disabilities and screen reader users lose their place when the page redirects, refreshes, or opens windows automatically without their request.
quickCheck: Confirm context changes happen only on explicit user action, and check there's no <meta http-equiv="refresh"> auto-redirect (axe meta-refresh-no-exceptions).
commonMistakes: Auto-refreshing or auto-redirecting with <meta http-equiv="refresh"> (axe meta-refresh-no-exceptions).
Opening new windows or popups the user didn't request.
Automatically advancing carousels or content in a way that changes context.

codeExamples.label: Context changes only on a user click
codeExamples.code:
{/* Navigation happens only when the user chooses */}
<button onClick={() => navigate("/dashboard")}>Go to dashboard</button>

codeExamples.label: Automatic redirect, not user-initiated
codeExamples.code:
<!-- Redirects on a timer rather than on user request -->
<meta http-equiv="refresh" content="5; url=/dashboard" />
